beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Hazel can't start the day without a swim, so she wakes early. Quick breakfast and prep before heading to the pool.
Short drive from Ballard to Green Lake Pool, her regular morning swim spot on this sunny Monday.
Hazel's essential start-of-day ritual; she swims laps and enjoys the early morning calm before work.
Drive south from Green Lake to Tukwila, arriving for a full day of wind turbine maintenance work.

beats.activity: Wind turbine maintenance and inspection work.
beats.reasoning: Full morning/early afternoon of technical work at the site; meticulous planner stays focused on the job despite the USA–Wales match at 11am, which she'll skip.

beats.activity: Lunch break at a nearby café.
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch near the site; sunny weather and early June warmth mean she grabs something light and returns to work.

beats.activity: Afternoon wind turbine work and site inspections.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift continues; meticulous technician completes the day's tasks before heading home.
Drive north from Tukwila back to Ballard; Light Rail closure (9am–3pm) doesn't affect this evening return.
Cooks or orders a simple dinner at home; easygoing personality means she unwinds after a full day of technical work.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: chess study and film photography review.
beats.reasoning: Hazel spends free time on chess and film photography; after a productive workday, she reviews photos from the morning swim or studies chess openings.
Full night sleep at home; Hazel rests before another early morning swim tomorrow.

diary: Woke up early to catch the sunrise swim at Green Lake—the water was perfect, that crisp cool feeling that washes away everything before the day starts. Got to the Tukwila site by nine and spent most of the morning checking blade connections on turbine 4. Grabbed a coffee and a maple bar at Drippy's around lunch; the place was buzzing with folks heading to bars downtown for that USA-Wales match. I caught myself thinking about it while up on the nacelle this afternoon—weird how the world keeps spinning down there while you're 200 feet up checking hydraulics. Made it home before the traffic got weird with the Light Rail down. Spent the evening reviewing some film negatives I shot last weekend and getting lost in a chess puzzle. There's something grounding about those two things together. Feeling tired but satisfied.
thought_process: The early swim was intentional—I needed that centering before a full day of climbs and inspections. I skipped the bars and the World Cup buzz; not really my scene, though I noticed the energy shift in town. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me today since I drive to Tukwila, but it made the commute smoother in a weird way. My mood stayed steady throughout—physical work does that for me. By evening, I was genuinely tired in that good way, so I kept things low-key with hobbies instead of forcing myself out. The weather helped too; that 74-degree sunshine made everything feel lighter.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
